mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals an abrupt labor market shock that compresses consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized real-economy expansion, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds the 95 index threshold, supply-side capacity constraints emerge, triggering input cost inflation that compresses equity profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when INDPRO breaches the threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential mortgage debt levels reflect expanding household leverage and credit availability, which directly fuels consumption-driven earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The series exhibits a negative correlation with forward SPX returns across all rolling 24-month windows, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.50.

mechanism_narrative: A twelve-month consumer price index increase surpassing 2.5 percent signals entrenched inflationary pressure that forces aggressive monetary tightening, elevating discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive despite CPI breaching the threshold, or the signal fails to predict rate hike cycles with at least 70% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and real GDP expansion confirms broad-based economic momentum, which sustains corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative rank correlation with forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for earnings seasonality.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in total nonfarm payrolls falling below 130,000 monthly additions indicates an employment recession that rapidly deteriorates consumer confidence and corporate revenue outlooks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where payrolls drop below the threshold, or the signal's hit rate does not exceed random chance by at least 15 percentage points.
